title: No session_id — tool calls, evolution logs, L1 writes can't be correlated within a session
what_failed: PiAgent has no session_id. Evolution log entries have no session grouping field. L1 raw_wiki writes generate a new random thread_id per write. This means there is no way to reconstruct what happened in a single session from logs — you can't answer 'what did Pi do in the 3pm session yesterday?' without guessing from timestamps.
where_failed: pi_agent.py __init__() — no session_id created. evolution.py log_interaction() — no session_id field. tools_memory.py memory_write() tier=l1 — random uuid per write.
why_likely: session_id was not in the original design. L1 was only recently implemented and used a random thread_id as a placeholder.
reproduction: Check evolution.jsonl — no session grouping field. Check raw_wiki entries — every thread_id is different even within the same session.
expected: PiAgent generates a session_id (short UUID) at startup. All evolution log entries include session_id. L1 writes use the session's thread_id.
actual: No session_id exists anywhere in the system.
suggested_fix: Add self.session_id = str(uuid.uuid4())[:8] in __init__(). Pass session_id to evolution.log_interaction() metadata. Pass to memory_write() as L1 thread_id via the MemoryTools constructor or as a method arg.
